language.label: Interface language
home.title: Turn a suspicious URL into an actionable case.
home.lead: Create the record, normalize the exact target, prepare the first actions and generate bilingual drafts. No network collection or external message occurs from this screen.
home.target_help: The exact path and query are preserved; fragments are excluded from collection.
home.safety.1: The input is normalized without contacting the target.
home.safety.2: A private local case record and SHA-256 manifest are created.
home.safety.3: Suggested actions and bilingual registrar drafts are prepared.
home.safety.4: A human must validate every fact before external use.
home.persistence: Cases are restored from integrity-checked local records after each restart. Private data remains under case-data/, which Git ignores.
home.load_warning: {count} local case(s) could not be restored because their integrity or format check failed.
case.pilot: Local pilot · Opening a case never contacts the target. Every collection requires explicit authorization.
case.overview.title: Case recorded
case.artifacts: {count} item(s) recorded in the evidence manifest.
case.evidence.title: Build the evidence package
case.evidence.intro: Collection starts only after your confirmation. Opening this step has no network effect.
case.evidence.authorized: I confirm that I am authorized to collect these elements.
case.evidence.scope: Bounded DNS, HTTP/TLS, RDAP and offline static rendering when available.
case.evidence.disabled: Technical collection is disabled in safe mode
case.evidence.manual: You can continue qualification using your manual observations.
case.evidence.launcher: Use the dedicated network launcher only for an authorized target.
case.evidence.reference: Reference snapshot: future checks will be compared with this one.
case.evidence.capture_caption: Offline derived rendering · JavaScript and network disabled
case.evidence.none: No technical snapshot has been recorded yet.
case.reporting.title: Prepare and perform reports
case.reporting.intro: Start with the recommended channel. The tool prepares the information and opens the destination, but submits nothing.
channel.icann_lookup.purpose: Identify the sponsoring registrar and its published abuse contact.
channel.icann_lookup.notes: Registrar Information can expose the registrar name, IANA ID, abuse email and abuse phone. Coverage varies for ccTLDs.
channel.icann_lookup.recommendation: Use this first to identify the registrar abuse contact.
channel.google_phishing.purpose: Report a page suspected of phishing or social engineering.
channel.google_phishing.notes: Use the exact page URL. Submission is manual and remains subject to Google's review.
channel.google_phishing.recommendation: Recommended when phishing or credential collection is observed.
channel.google_malware.purpose: Report a page suspected of distributing malware or unwanted software.
channel.google_malware.notes: Choose this channel for observed malware or unwanted-software behavior, not ordinary impersonation alone.
channel.google_malware.recommendation: Recommended only for observed malware behavior.
channel.microsoft_unsafe_site.purpose: Submit one or more unsafe URLs for Microsoft review.
channel.microsoft_unsafe_site.notes: Microsoft states that corporate account holders can report multiple URLs in one submission.
channel.microsoft_unsafe_site.recommenda: General user-protection review for unsafe URLs.
channel.pharos_fr.purpose: Report potentially illegal internet content to French authorities.
channel.pharos_fr.notes: PHAROS is not an emergency service. The operator must decide whether the observed content falls within the official scope.
channel.pharos_fr.recommendation: Human decision required: use only when the official scope applies.
workflow.evidence.attention: The latest attempt requires your attention.
workflow.evidence.disabled: Collection unavailable in the current safe mode.
workflow.qualification.required: Human validation of the observations is required.
workflow.reporting.ready: Channels and drafts are ready for review.
workflow.follow_up.waiting: Follow-up starts after the first report.
workflow.follow_up.complete: Processing of this case is complete.
workflow.follow_up.due: A follow-up or new check is due.
workflow.next.evidence.title: Build the technical evidence package
workflow.next.qualification.title: Confirm qualification
workflow.next.reporting.title: Prepare the next report
workflow.next.reporting.detail: Choose the recommended channel, then use the prepared text.
workflow.next.follow_up.title: Follow up on actions taken
case.qualification.title: Qualify observations
case.qualification.brand.title: The selected brand is represented
case.qualification.brand.help: Logo, name, identity or unambiguous reference.
case.qualification.copy.title: Products, text or visuals are copied
case.qualification.copy.help: The content reproduces elements from the legitimate site.
case.qualification.sensitive.title: A login, data-entry or payment path is offered
case.qualification.sensitive.help: The page requests sensitive information or a transaction.
case.qualification.victims.title: Victims or transactions are known
case.qualification.victims.help: Confirmed reports exist beyond the page itself.
case.qualification.related.title: A related case or campaign is known
case.qualification.related.help: A recurrence or relationship has been identified.
case.qualification.public.title: The target was accessible during review
case.qualification.public.help: Dated human observation, without an automatic network check.
case.qualification.reason_help: Required only when criticality differs from the proposal
case.reporting.copy_summary: Copy a summary for a form
case.reporting.email: Prepare an email to the registrar
case.reporting.recipient_help: The address is used only to open your email client and is not stored.
case.reporting.subject: Subject
case.reporting.record: I submitted a report: record the result
case.reporting.record_help: Use only after the report was actually sent through the official website or your email client.
case.reporting.confirm_sent: I confirm that this report was actually sent outside the tool.
case.follow_up.title: Follow up on actions
case.follow_up.not_due: No action is required before this date.
case.follow_up.empty: Follow-up will be scheduled automatically after the first report is recorded.
case.details.title: Case journal and secondary tools
case.details.summary: {count} event(s) · compliance details and internal actions
